items.title: Pinning a fast-moving SDK
items.scenario: Your code uses the openai 1.x client API and must not silently upgrade to a breaking release.
items.explanation: openai==1.40.0 in requirements.txt locks the version so installs are deterministic.

items.title: Recreating an environment
items.scenario: A teammate or CI needs the exact set of packages your project uses.
items.explanation: pip install -r requirements.txt installs the listed dependencies in one step.

items.title: Separating dev tools
items.scenario: pytest and ruff are needed for development but not in production.
items.explanation: Keep them in dev-requirements.txt and include it from requirements only where needed.

items.title: Installing extras
items.scenario: A library offers optional features behind an extra.
items.explanation: package[extra] pulls in the optional dependency group only when you ask for it.
